name: AI-Hydro Tools
author: AI-Hydro
description: Core hydrological research tools: watershed delineation, streamflow analysis, climate forcing, terrain analysis, differentiable modelling, and more.
readmeContent:
# AI-Hydro Tools

The Python backend for the AI-Hydro platform — a collection of hydrological and geospatial analysis tools exposed via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## Installation

```bash
pip install aihydro-tools
```

This installs all tools and the `aihydro-mcp` server command.

## Available Tool Categories

- **Watershed**: NHDPlus delineation from USGS NLDI
- **Streamflow**: USGS NWIS daily discharge retrieval
- **Signatures**: 15+ hydrological flow statistics (BFI, runoff ratio, FDC)
- **Geomorphic**: 28 basin morphometry metrics
- **Terrain**: Topographic Wetness Index from 3DEP DEM
- **Curve Number**: NRCS CN grid from NLCD + Polaris soils
- **Forcing**: GridMET basin-averaged climate data
- **CAMELS**: Full CAMELS-US attribute set via pygeohydro
- **Knowledge**: RAG search over hydrological literature
- **Modelling**: Differentiable HBV-light and NeuralHydrology LSTM
- **Session**: Research session management with caching and provenance

## Quick Start

After installation, the AI-Hydro VS Code extension auto-detects and registers the MCP server. You can also run it manually:

```bash
aihydro-mcp
```

## Plugin System

Community packages can register additional tools via entry points:

```toml
[project.entry-points."aihydro.tools"]
my_tool = "my_package.tools:my_tool_function"
```

## Links

- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/aihydro-tools/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/AI-Hydro/aihydro-tools)
- [AI-Hydro Platform](https://github.com/AI-Hydro/AI-Hydro)
llmsInstallationContent:
## Installation Instructions

1. Run in terminal: `pip install aihydro-tools`
2. Verify: `aihydro-mcp` should start and list 17 registered tools.
3. Restart AI-Hydro. The extension auto-detects `aihydro-mcp` (checks PATH and common pip install directories).
4. If not detected, add manually to `aihydro_mcp_settings.json` — see README for paths per OS.

Optional extras: `pip install aihydro-tools[data]` (geo retrieval), `pip install aihydro-tools[analysis]` (terrain/watershed), `pip install aihydro-tools[all]` (everything).

Note: Requires Python 3.9+ and pip. Conda environments are supported.
